text:
The following is the full text of a technical specification. Please read it and produce a one-paragraph summary at the end.

Specification: A high-throughput LLM inference system must balance memory bandwidth, compute, and KV-cache scheduling. The KV cache stores attention keys and values produced during the prefill phase and reused during decode. As batch size grows, KV-cache memory grows linearly, becoming the dominant memory consumer for long-context workloads. Quantization of KV-cache (e.g. FP8) halves this footprint, allowing higher concurrent batch sizes. Speculative decoding amortizes the decode-step memory bandwidth by emitting multiple candidate tokens per verification pass. Combined, the two techniques can boost effective throughput by 2-3x on bandwidth-limited hardware. The choice of decoding strategy interacts with the chunked-prefill scheduler; aggressive speculation can starve new prefill chunks. A well-tuned configuration balances these. Continuous batching keeps the GPU utilized as requests complete or arrive. Each of these knobs has measurable impact on tokens-per-second-per-user, time-to-first-token, and inter-token latency. The optimization surface is multidimensional and requires empirical sweeping rather than analytical solution. Now please produce a one-paragraph summary.

Please respond to the following lengthy scenario. A team is operating an LLM inference platform on workstation-class Blackwell GPUs. They need to choose between FP8 and NVFP4 quantization for a 35B MoE model. The hardware has 128GB unified memory at 273 GB/s bandwidth. The team has measured: NVFP4 on the default container yields 12 tok/s single-request; FP8 yields 53 tok/s with no speculative decoding, and 61 tok/s with MTP-1 speculative decoding. The MoE model has 3B activated parameters per token. About 70% of the model's layers are linear-attention (Mamba-style) and 30% are standard attention. The team's workload is mixed: some low-latency chat, some batch processing. Based on these facts, write a recommendation paragraph that justifies which quantization + speculative configuration the team should deploy.

Consider the following situation. A researcher is comparing speculative decoding methods on Qwen3.6-35B-A3B running under vLLM 0.17.1 on consumer Blackwell hardware. They want to enable speculative decoding to lift single-request throughput. Available methods include: MTP (multi-token prediction, with the head shipped in the model checkpoint), EAGLE-3 (requires a separately-trained draft model), and DFlash (block-diffusion drafter). The researcher reports: no EAGLE-3 draft model has been published for this specific MoE+linear-attention architecture; the DFlash drafter exists but its acceptance rate collapses under FP8 quantization, with only 4/15 speculated tokens accepted; MTP-1 yields 90% acceptance on representative prompts. MTP-2 has been observed to fail at concurrency 8. Based on this evidence, write a brief recommendation justifying which speculative method to enable.

Long context test. The following is a fictional log of a system administrator setting up a vLLM container. 

[INFO] Starting vLLM container with image nvcr.io/nvidia/vllm:26.03.post1-py3
[INFO] Loading model Qwen/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-FP8 from local cache
[INFO] Quantization: fp8
[INFO] Max model length: 262144
[INFO] Max num batched tokens: 16384
[INFO] GPU memory utilization: 0.85
[INFO] KV cache dtype: fp8
[INFO] Reasoning parser: qwen3
[INFO] Tool call parser: qwen3_xml
[INFO] Speculative config: {"method":"mtp","num_speculative_tokens":1}
[INFO] Loading 42 safetensors shards...
[INFO] All shards loaded
[INFO] CUDA graph warmup begin
[INFO] CUDA graph warmup end (took 124 seconds)
[INFO] vLLM serving on http://0.0.0.0:8000/v1/
[INFO] First request: 245ms
[INFO] Second request: 89ms
[INFO] Tenth request: 73ms
[INFO] Steady-state: 73-89ms per request

Given this log, write a one-paragraph summary of what the operator should report to their team.
